hints.Nihil amplius: 'Nihil amplius' means 'nothing more' — it is the object, not the verb
hints.amplius quam: 'amplius quam' means 'more than' — these are comparative words, not a verb
hints.Nihil est: 'Nihil' is a pronoun and 'est' is part of the verb, but 'constitutum est' is the full compound verb

steps.prompt: The infinitive of 'constitutum est' translates to?

hints.to stand together: 'to stand together' is a literal reading of 'con + statuere', but 'constituere' means 'to establish/set up'
hints.to constitute (modern sense): The modern English sense diverges — Latin 'constituere' means 'to establish/ordain'

steps.prompt: What tense and voice is 'constitutum est'?

hints.Present passive: Present passive would be 'constituitur' — 'constitutum est' (participle + est) is perfect passive
hints.Future passive: Future passive would be 'constituetur' — 'constitutum est' is perfect passive
hints.Imperfect passive: Imperfect passive would be 'constituebatur' — 'constitutum est' is perfect passive

hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — a 1st conjugation verb
hints.statuo, statuere, statui, statutum: These are the parts of 'statuere' (to set up) — 'constituere' adds the prefix con-
hints.instituo, instituere, institui, institut: These are the parts of 'instituere' (to set up/institute) — different prefix

hints.Not: 'not' is 'non' — 'nihil' is an indeclinable pronoun meaning 'nothing'
hints.No one: 'no one' is 'nemo' — 'nihil' means 'nothing' (a thing, not a person)

hints.as much as: 'as much as' is 'tantum quantum' — 'amplius quam' means 'more than'
hints.less than: 'less than' is 'minus quam' — 'amplius' is the comparative of 'ample' (more)
hints.other than: 'other than' is 'aliter quam' — 'amplius quam' means 'more than'

translation: Nothing more than what has been appointed
